raw_event.description: 84% tariff on Chinese integrated circuits and advanced semiconductors under Section 301

enriched_event.description: 84% tariff on Chinese integrated circuits and advanced semiconductors under Section 301
key_facts: CHIPS Act domestic exemptions apply to HTS 8542.31 and 8542.39

top_sku_actions.action: Switch to domestic supplier
Switch to domestic supplier
Switch to domestic supplier
Switch to domestic supplier

pros: Elimination of 84% tariff on affected SKUs
25% tax credit from CHIPS Act for qualified suppliers
cons: Higher labor costs due to 2.3x labor premium in the US
Potential supply chain disruptions during the transition period
scenarios.summary: Reshoring affected SKUs to US-based suppliers can eliminate the 84% tariff and reduce annual tariff impact by $46.7 million, while incurring a labor premium cost of $11.34 million, resulting in a net annual cost delta of $11.34 million. The top SKUs to consider for reshoring are IC-8542-001, MEM-8542-006, IC-8541-002, and IC-8542-004, which account for 75% of the supplier coverage. The lead time for reshoring is estimated to be 12 months, and the risk score is 0.2, indicating a relatively low risk.

top_sku_actions.action: diversify
qualify

scenarios.summary: We recommend prioritizing Mexico maquiladoras for high-tariff items, resulting in a 10-month lead time and $12.4M annual cost increase. The nearshore strategy mitigates 26% of total tariff impact, with a 6.2 risk score and 85% confidence level.

top_sku_actions.action: split 65% to GlobalFoundries (US) and 35% to TSMC Manufacturing
split 65% to Micron (US) and 35% to ChangXin Memory (China)
split 65% to Texas Instruments (US) and 35% to Analog Devices China
split 65% to Qualcomm (US) and 35% to MediaTek China

scenarios.summary: Implementing a dual-source strategy with a 65/35 split between exempt and Chinese suppliers can reduce annual tariff impact by $2,358,144 and increase supply chain resilience, but may also increase management costs and lead times.

top_sku_actions.action: split 65% to GlobalFoundries (US) and 35% to TSMC Manufacturing
split 65% to Micron (US) and 35% to ChangXin Memory (China)
split 65% to Texas Instruments (US) and 35% to Analog Devices China

ranked_scenarios.summary: Implementing a dual-source strategy with a 65/35 split between exempt and Chinese suppliers can reduce annual tariff impact by $2,358,144 and increase supply chain resilience, but may also increase management costs and lead times.
ranked_scenarios.recommendation_rationale: Dual-source strategy offers the best outcome due to its negative annual cost delta, indicating cost savings, and high supplier coverage percentage.

emails.subject: Notification of Re-Sourcing Decision for SKU IC-8542-001
emails.body: Dear GlobalFoundries (US) team, we are writing to inform you that due to the recent tariff event USTR-2026-04-SEMI-001, we have decided to implement a dual-source strategy for SKU IC-8542-001. As a result, we will be splitting 65% of our orders for this SKU to your company, with the remaining 35% to be sourced from TSMC Manufacturing. We request that you qualify for this new volume within the next 12 weeks. Please respond to this email within 5 business days to confirm your ability to meet this new demand.

emails.subject: Notification of Re-Sourcing Decision for SKU MEM-8542-006
emails.body: Dear Micron (US) team, we are writing to inform you that due to the recent tariff event USTR-2026-04-SEMI-001, we have decided to implement a dual-source strategy for SKU MEM-8542-006. As a result, we will be splitting 65% of our orders for this SKU to your company, with the remaining 35% to be sourced from ChangXin Memory (China). We request that you qualify for this new volume within the next 12 weeks. Please respond to this email within 5 business days to confirm your ability to meet this new demand.

We request that you qualify for this new volume within the next 12 weeks. Please respond to this email within 5 business days to confirm your ability to meet this new demand.
